user_query:
I’m preparing a quick summary from my students’ neighborhood fieldwork and a short note to my principal. Please do the following using only the provided files:

1) Write and run a small script (any language is fine) that reads input/student_fieldwork.csv, treats all records with district == "Longhua" as part of "Bao'an" (merge Longhua into Bao'an for all grouping and totals), computes per-district aggregates, and saves output/summary_by_district.csv with the columns:
- district
- sample_size (count of rows)
- avg_litter_count (mean, rounded to 2 decimals)
- median_hours_spent (median, rounded to 2 decimals)
- total_observations (sum)
- avg_water_ph (mean, rounded to 2 decimals)
Include a final citywide row labeled district = "All" computed after the Longhua→Bao'an merge. Save your script at scripts/analyze.py (or equivalent) and run it to produce the CSV.

2) Rewrite input/draft_email.txt into a concise, professional note at output/email_to_principal.txt (max 120 words). The rewritten note must include the following numbers exactly as computed and rounded in the CSV:
- Bao'an (including Longhua): sample_size and avg_litter_count
- Nanshan: sample_size and avg_litter_count
- All (citywide): sample_size and avg_litter_count

Assume Longhua is part of Bao'an throughout. Don’t use any external data; base all figures on the CSV provided. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
